summarization.a: The figure shows XPS spectra of Mo 3d // S 2p for pristine bilayer and after Cl adsorption.
summarization.b: The figure displays XPS spectra of Cl 2p for pristine bilayer and various Ar exposure times.
summarization.c: The figure presents XPS spectra of Mo 3d // S 2p after Cl adsorption and Ar exposure times.
summarization.d: The figure shows XPS spectra of Mo 3d // S 2p after several Ar exposure times.
summarization.e: The figure displays XPS spectra of Mo 3d // S 2p after several Ar exposure times.
summarization.f: The figure presents XPS spectra of Mo 3d // S 2p after Ar exposure times and for a pristine bilayer.

a.question: Does the chlorine adsorption change the peak ratios of the Mo 3d peak?
a.answer: a	This only happens slightly, however the S-Mo-S peak does seem to be removed more, indicating that the sulfur is no longer bonded to Mo.

c.question: Which atoms are selectively etched?
c.answer: c	The sulfur peaks decrease whereas the molybdenum peaks remain constant, so the sulfur is selectively etched.

e.question: Why do the peak intensities increase upon longer plasma exposure after 60s?
e.answer: e	The intensities of a bilayer MoS2 are equal to those of a Monolayer, so the should converge back to the pristine one.

b.question: Is the chlorine selectively etched?
b.answer: b	All peaks are equally removed by the argon plasma, so no this is not the case.
